We intentionally do this\\n383: \\t// _after_ writing the state because we want to check against\\n384: \\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n385: \\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n386: \\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\\n387: }\\n388: \\n389: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourcePostconditions(ctx EvalContext, repeatData instances.RepetitionData) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n390: \\n391: \\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n392: \\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n393: \\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n394: \\t\\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\\n395: \\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n396: \\t)\\n397: \\treturn diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n398: }\\n399: \\n400: // checkPlannedChange produces errors if the _actual_ expected value is not\\n401: // compatible with what was recorded in the plan.\\n402: //\\n403: // Errors here are most often indicative of a bug in the provider, so our error\\n404: // messages will report with that in mind. It's also possible that there's a bug\\n405: // in Terraform's Core's own \\\"proposed new value\\\" code in EvalDiff.\\n406: func (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) checkPlannedChange(ctx EvalContext, plannedChange, actualChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n407: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n408: \\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\\n409: \\n410: \\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.ContainingResource())\\n411: \\tif schema == nil {\\n412: \\t\\t// Should be caught during \\t}\\n416: \\n417: \\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(ctx.Path())\\n418: \\n419: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] checkPlannedChange: Verifying that actual change (action %s) matches planned change (action %s)\\\", actualChange.Action, plannedChange.Action)\\n420: \\n421: \\tif plannedChange.Action != actualChange.Action {\\n422: \\t\\tswitch {\\n423: \\t\\tcase plannedChange.Action == plans.Update && actualChange.Action == plans.NoOp:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t// It's okay for an update to become a NoOp once we've filled in\\n425: \\t\\t\\t// all of the unknown values, since the final values might actually\\n426: \\t\\t\\t// match what was there before after all.\\n427: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] After incorporating new values learned so far during apply, %s change has become NoOp\\\", absAddr)\\n428: \\n429: \\t\\tcase (plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete && actualChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate) ||\\n430: \\t\\t\\t(plannedChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate && actualChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete):\\n431: \\t\\t\\t// If the order of replacement changed, then that is a bug in terraform\\n432: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n433: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n434: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Terraform produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n435: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n436: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, plannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\\n438: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n439: \\t\\t\\t))\\n440: \\t\\tdefault:\\n441: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n442: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n443: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n444: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n445: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q changed the planned action from %s to %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n446: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(),\\n447: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tplannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\\n448: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n449: \\t\\t\\t))\\n450: \\t\\t}\\n451: \\t}\\n452: \\n453: \\terrs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, plannedChange.After, actualChange.After)\\n454: \\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n455: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n456: \\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n457: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\\\",\\n458: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\n459: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q produced an invalid new value for %s.\\\\n\\\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\\\",\\n460: \\t\\t\\t\\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\\n461: \\t\\t\\t),\\n462: \\t\\t))\\n463: \\t}\\n464: \\treturn diags\\n465: }\\n466: \\n467: // maybeTainted takes the resource addr, new value, planned change, and possible\\n468: // error from an apply operation and return a new instance object marked as\\n469: // tainted if it appears that a create operation has failed.\\n470: func maybeTainted(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, err error) We'll report the deferral and\\n401: \\t\\t\\t// store what we could produce in the deferral tracker.\\n402: \\t\\t\\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\\n403: \\t\\t} else if !deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\\n404: \\t\\t\\t// We intentionally write the change before the subsequent checks, because\\n405: \\t\\t\\t// all of the checks below this point are for problems caused by the\\n406: \\t\\t\\t// context surrounding the change, rather than the change itself, and\\n407: \\t\\t\\t// so it's helpful to still include the valid-in-isolation change as\\n408: \\t\\t\\t// part of the plan as additional context in our error output.\\n409: \\t\\t\\t//\\n410: \\t\\t\\t// FIXME: it is currently important that we write resource changes to\\n411: \\t\\t\\t// the plan (n.writeChange) before we write the corresponding state\\n412: \\t\\t\\t// (n.writeResourceInstanceState).\\n413: \\t\\t\\t//\\n414: \\t\\t\\t// This is because the planned resource state will normally have the\\n415: \\t\\t\\t// status of states.ObjectPlanned, which causes later logic to refer to\\n416: \\t\\t\\t// the contents of the plan to retrieve the resource data. Because\\n417: \\t\\t\\t// there is no shared lock between these two data structures, reversing\\n418: \\t\\t\\t// the order of these writes will cause a brief window of inconsistency\\n419: \\t\\t\\t// which can lead to a failed safety check.\\n420: \\t\\t\\t//\\n421: \\t\\t\\t// Future work should adjust these APIs such that it is impossible to\\n422: \\t\\t\\t// update these two data structures incorrectly through any objects\\n423: \\t\\t\\t// reachable via the terraform.EvalContext API.\\n424: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \\\"\\\"))\\n425: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n427: \\t\\t\\t}\\n428: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instancePlanState, workingState))\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\n433: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPreventDestroy(change))\\n434: \\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n435: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n436: \\t\\t\\t}\\n437: \\n438: \\t\\t\\t// If this plan resulted in a NoOp, then apply won't have a chance to make\\n439: \\t\\t\\t// any changes to the stored dependencies. Since this is a NoOp we know\\n440: \\t\\t\\t// that the stored dependencies will have no effect during apply, and we can\\n441: \\t\\t\\t// write them out now.\\n442: \\t\\t\\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp && !depsEqual(instanceRefreshState.Dependencies, n.Dependencies) {\\n443: \\t\\t\\t\\t// the refresh state will be the final state for this resource, so\\n444: \\t\\t\\t\\t// finalize the dependencies here if they need to be updated.\\n445: \\t\\t\\t\\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\\n446: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\\n447: \\t\\t\\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n448: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n449: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n450: \\t\\t\\t}\\n451: \\n452: \\t\\t\\t// Post-conditions might block completion. We intentionally do this\\n453: \\t\\t\\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\\n454: \\t\\t\\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\\n455: \\t\\t\\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\\n456: \\t\\t\\t// (Note that some preconditions will end up being skipped during\\n457: \\t\\t\\t// planning, because their conditions depend on values not yet known.)\\n458: \\t\\t\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n459: \\t\\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n460: \\t\\t\\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n461: \\t\\t\\t\\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\\n462: \\t\\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n463: \\t\\t\\t)\\n464: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n465: \\t\\t} else {\\n466: \\t\\t\\t// The deferrals tracker says that we must defer changes for\\n467: \\t\\t\\t// this resource instance, presumably due to a dependency on an\\n468: \\t\\t\\t// upstream object that was already deferred. Therefore we just\\n469: \\t\\t\\t// report our own deferral (capturing a placeholder value in the\\n470: \\t\\t\\t// deferral tracker) and don't add anything to the plan or\\n471: \\t\\t\\t// working state.\\n472: \\t\\t\\t// In this case, the expression evaluator should use the placeholder\\n473: \\t\\t\\t// value registered here as the value of this resource instance,\\n474: \\t\\t\\t// instead of using the plan.\\n475: \\t\\t\\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)\\n476: \\t\\t}\\n477: \\t} else {\\n478: \\t\\t// In refresh-only mode we need to evaluate the for-each expression in\\n479: \\t\\t// order to supply the value to the pre- and post-condition check\\n480: \\t\\t// blocks. This has the unfortunate edge case of a refresh-only plan\\n481: \\t\\t// executing with a for-each map which has the same keys but different\\n482: \\t\\t// values, which could result in a post-condition check relying on that\\n483: \\t\\t// value being inaccurate. Unless we decide to store the value of the\\n484: \\t\\t// for-each expression in state, this is unavoidable.\\n485: \\t\\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n486: \\t\\trepeatData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n487: \\n488: \\t\\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\\n489: \\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\\n490: \\t\\t\\tn.Config.Preconditions,\\n491: \\t\\t\\tctx, addr, repeatData,\\n492: \\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n493: \\t\\t)\\n494: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n495: \\n496: \\t\\t// Even if we don't plan changes, we do still need to at least update\\n497: \\t\\t// the working state to reflect the refresh result. If not, then e.g.\\n498: \\t\\t// any output values refering to this will not react to the drift.\\n499: \\t\\t// (Even if we didn't actually refresh above, this will still save\\n500: \\t\\t// the result of any schema upgrading we did in readResourceInstanceState.)\\n501: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, workingState))\\n502: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n503: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n504: \\t\\t}\\n505: \\n506: \\t\\t// Here we also evaluate post-conditions after updating the working\\n507: \\t\\t// state, because we want to check against the result of the refresh.\\n508: \\t\\t// Unlike in normal planning mode, these checks are still evaluated\\n509: \\t\\t// even if pre-conditions generated diagnostics, because we have no\\n510: \\t\\t// planned changes to block.\\n511: \\t\\tcheckDiags = evalCheckRules(\\n512: \\t\\t\\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\\n513: \\t\\t\\tn.Config.Postconditions,\\n514: \\t\\t\\tctx, addr, repeatData,\\n515: \\t\\t\\tcheckRuleSeverity,\\n516: \\t\\t)\\n517: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\\n518: \\n519: \\t\\t// In this case we skipped planning changes and therefore need to report the deferral\\n520: \\t\\t// here, if there was one.\\n521: \\t\\tif refreshDeferred != nil {\\n522: \\t\\t\\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\\n523: \\t\\t\\t\\tAddr:         n.Addr,\\n524: \\t\\t\\t\\tPrevRunAddr:  n.Addr,\\n525: \\t\\t\\t\\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\\n526: \\t\\t\\t\\tChange: plans.Change{\\n527: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAction: plans.Read,\\n528: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tBefore: priorInstanceRefreshState.Value,\\n529: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAfter:  instanceRefreshState.Value,\\n530: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n531: \\t\\t\\t})\\n532: \\t\\t}\\n533: \\t}\\n534: \\n535: \\treturn diags\\n536: }\\n537: \\n538: // replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change\\n539: // in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the\\n540: // instance address is added to forceReplace\\n541: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\\n542: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n543: \\tif n.Config == nil {\\n544: \\t\\treturn diags\\n545: \\t}\\n546: \\n547: \\tfor _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {\\n548: \\t\\tref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)\\n549: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\\n550: \\t\\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n551: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n552: \\t\\t}\\n553: \\n554: \\t\\tif replace {\\n555: \\t\\t\\t// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may\\n556: \\t\\t\\t// want to communicate more information about which resource\\n557: \\t\\t\\t// triggered the replacement in the plan.\\n558: \\t\\t\\t// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more\\n559: \\t\\t\\t// options, we can refactor both of these features later.\\n560: \\t\\t\\tn.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)\\n561: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s\\\", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())\\n562: \\n563: \\t\\t\\tn.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)\\n564: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n565: \\t\\t}\\n566: \\t}\\n567: \\n568: \\treturn diags\\n569: }\\n570: \\n571: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) importState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, importId string, provider providers.Interface, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n572: \\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\\n573: \\tvar diags already happened.\\n606: \\t\\tif n.Config == nil {\\n607: \\t\\t\\t// But, just in case we change this at some point in the future,\\n608: \\t\\t\\t// let's add a specific error message here we can This shouldn't happen in\\n610: \\t\\t\\t// production, so we don't bother with a pretty error.\\n611: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\\\"override blocks do not support config generation\\\"))\\n612: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, deferred, diags\\n613: \\t\\t}\\n614: \\n615: \\t\\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\\n616: \\t\\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\\n617: \\t\\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.Config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\\n618: \\t\\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\\n619: \\t\\t\\t// We have an overridden resource so we're definitely in a test and\\n620: \\t\\t\\t// the users config is not valid. generateHCLStringAttributes produces a string in HCL format for the given\\n835: // resource state and schema without the surrounding block.\\n836: func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) generateHCLStringAttributes(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, schema *configschema.Block) (string, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n837: \\tfilteredSchema := schema.Filter(\\n838: \\t\\tconfigschema.FilterOr(\\n839: \\t\\t\\tconfigschema.FilterReadOnlyAttribute,\\n840: \\t\\t\\tconfigschema.FilterDeprecatedAttribute,\\n841: \\n842: \\t\\t\\t// The legacy SDK adds an Optional+Computed \\\"id\\\" attribute to the\\n843: \\t\\t\\t// resource schema even if not defined in provider code.\\n844: \\t\\t\\t// During validation, however, the presence of an *states.State\\n23: \\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\\n24: \\tConfig   *configs.Config\\n25: }\\n26: \\n27: // return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\\n28: // Postconditions defined in the configuration.\\n29: func (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\\n30: \\t// unit tests may have no config\\n31: \\tif t.Config \\t}\\n53: \\n54: \\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\\n55: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\\\")\\n56: \\n57: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n58: \\tstate := edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\\n65: \\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\\n66: \\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\\n73: \\t\\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\\n74: \\t\\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\\\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\\n88: \\n89: \\t\\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\\n90: \\t\\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\\n91: \\t\\t// other actions.\\n92: \\t\\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\\n93: \\t\\tswitch rc.Action {\\n94: \\t\\tcase plans.NoOp:\\n95: \\t\\t\\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\\n96: \\t\\t\\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\\n97: \\t\\t\\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\\n98: \\t\\t\\t// results of other changes.\\n99: \\t\\t\\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\\n100: \\t\\tcase plans.Delete:\\n101: \\t\\t\\tdelete = true\\n102: \\t\\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\\n103: \\t\\t\\tupdate = true\\n104: \\t\\t\\tdelete = true\\n105: \\t\\t\\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\\n106: \\t\\tcase plans.Forget:\\n107: \\t\\t\\tforget = true\\n108: \\t\\tdefault:\\n109: \\t\\t\\tupdate = true\\n110: \\t\\t}\\n111: \\n112: \\t\\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\\n113: \\t\\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\\n114: \\t\\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\\n115: \\t\\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\\n116: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n118: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\\\",\\n119: \\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\\\", addr, dk),\\n120: \\t\\t\\t))\\n121: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n122: \\t\\t}\\n123: \\n124: \\t\\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\\n125: \\t\\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\\n126: \\t\\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\\n127: \\t\\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\\n128: \\t\\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\\n129: \\t\\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\\n131: \\t\\t\\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\\n132: \\t\\t\\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\\n133: \\t\\t\\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\\n134: \\t\\t\\t// object we expect.\\n135: \\t\\t\\tif state != nil {\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\\n137: \\t\\t\\t\\tif ris == nil {\\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\\n139: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\\n140: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\\n141: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ttfdiags.Error,\\n142: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Invalid planned change\\\",\\n143: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfmt.Sprintf(\\\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. If we\\n293: \\t\\t\\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\\n294: \\t\\t\\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\\n295: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n297: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Configuration for import target does not exist\\\",\\n298: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. If you wish to automatically generate config for this resource, use the -generate-config-out option within terraform plan. Otherwise, make sure the target resource exists within your configuration. For example:\\\\n\\\\n  terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf\\\", n.Addr),\\n299: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  n.importTargets[0].Config.To.Range().Ptr(),\\n300: \\t\\t\\t})\\n301: \\t\\t\\treturn diags\\n302: \\t\\t}\\n303: \\t}\\n304: \\n305: \\treturn diags\\n306: }\\n307: \\n308: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\\n309: \\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\\n310: \\t\\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\\n311: \\t\\treturn nil\\n312: \\t}\\n313: \\n314: \\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\\n315: \\n316: \\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\\n317: \\tsMgr := ctx.State()\\n318: \\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\\n319: \\n320: \\tfor _, res \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n326: \\t\\t\\t}\\n327: \\t\\t}\\n328: \\t\\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\\n329: \\t\\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n340: \\tvar g Graph\\n341: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n342: \\n343: \\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\\n344: \\n345: \\tfor _, res := range orphans {\\n346: \\t\\tfor key := range \\t\\t}\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n373: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n374: \\t}\\n375: \\n376: \\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\\n377: \\n378: \\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\\n379: \\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\\n380: \\t// \\n387: \\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\\n388: \\n389: \\treturn &g, diags\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: // expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\\n393: // itself in the context of a particular module instance.\\n394: //\\n395: // It has several side-effects:\\n396: //   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\\n397: //   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \\\"expander\\\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\\n398: //   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\\n399: //\\n400: // After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\\n401: // within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\\n402: // checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\\n403: // object instances for this resource instance.\\n404: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n405: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n406: \\n407: \\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\\n408: \\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\\n409: \\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\\n410: \\n411: \\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\\n412: \\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\\n413: \\t// to work below.\\n414: \\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\\n415: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n416: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n417: \\t\\treturn nil, diags\\n418: \\t}\\n419: \\n420: \\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\\n421: \\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\\n422: \\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\\n423: \\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\\n424: \\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\\n425: \\texpander {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PgLB8as49y6Pu7WD9B7bKY","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\\n2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\\n3: \\n4: package terraform\\n5: \\n6: import (\\n7: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\\\"\\n8: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\\\"\\n9: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\\\"\\n10: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\\\"\\n11: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\\\"\\n12: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\\\"\\n13: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\\\"\\n14: \\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\\\"\\n15: )\\n16: \\n17: // ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\\n18: // a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\\n19: //\\n20: // Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\\n21: // this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\\n22: // that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\\n23: // diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\\n24: type ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\\n25: \\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\\n26: \\tConfig *configs.Config\\n27: \\n28: \\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\\n29: \\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\\n30: \\n31: \\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\\n32: \\t// and should not be applied.\\n33: \\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\\n34: \\n35: \\t// State is the current state\\n36: \\tState *states.State\\n37: \\n38: \\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\\n39: \\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\\n40: \\t// to get a consistent result.\\n41: \\tRootVariableValues InputValues\\n42: \\n43: \\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\\n44: \\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\\n45: \\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\\n46: \\t// initialization step.\\n47: \\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\\n48: \\n49: \\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\\n50: \\t// provisioners) available for use.\\n51: \\tPlugins *contextPlugins\\n52: \\n53: \\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\\n54: \\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\\n55: \\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\\n56: \\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\\n57: \\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\\n58: \\n59: \\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\\n60: \\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\\n61: \\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\\n62: \\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\\n63: \\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\\n64: \\n65: \\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\\n66: \\tOperation walkOperation\\n67: \\n68: \\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\\n69: \\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\\n70: \\t// the actual graph.\\n71: \\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\\n72: \\n73: \\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\\n74: \\t// framework.\\n75: \\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: // See GraphBuilder\\n79: func (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n80: \\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\\n81: \\t\\tSteps: b.Steps(),\\n82: GraphNodeReferencer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\t// If we have a config then we prefer to use that.\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\t\tresult = append(result, n.DependsOn()...)\n\n\t\tif n.Schema == nil {\n\t\t\t// Should never happen, but we'll log if it does so that we can\n\t\t\t// see this easily when != nil {\n\t\t\trefs, _ = langrefs.ReferencesInBlock(addrs.ParseRef, c.Config, n.Schema)\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, refs...)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif c.Managed != nil {\n\t\t\tif c.Managed.Connection != DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference {\n\tvar result []*addrs.Reference\n\tif c := n.Config; c != nil {\n\n\t\tfor _, traversal := range c.DependsOn {\n\t\t\tref, diags := addrs.ParseRef(traversal)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t// We ignore this here, because this isn't a suitable place to return\n\t\t\t\t// errors. This situation should be caught and rejected during\n\t\t\t\t// validation.\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] Can't parse %#v from depends_on as reference: %s\", traversal, diags.Err())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, ref)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) SetProvider(p addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = p\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// Once the provider is fully resolved, we can return the known value.\n\tif n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.ResolvedProvider, true\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have a config we prefer that above all else\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\trelAddr := n.Config.ProviderConfigAddr()\n\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\tLocalName: relAddr.LocalName,\n\t\t\tAlias:     relAddr.Alias,\n\t\t}, false\n\t}\n\n\t// See if we have a valid provider config from the state.\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\t// An address from the state must match exactly, since we must ensure\n\t\t// we refresh/destroy a resource with the same provider configuration\n\t\t// that created it.\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We might have an import target that is providing a specific provider,\n\t// this is okay as we know there is nothing else potentially providing a\n\t// provider configuration.\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil && n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef != nil {\n\t\t\treturn addrs.LocalProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tLocalName: n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Name,\n\t\t\t\tAlias:     n.importTargets[0].Config.ProviderConfigRef.Alias,\n\t\t\t}, false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// No provider configuration found; return a default address\n\treturn addrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\tProvider: n.Provider(),\n\t\tModule:   n.ModulePath(),\n\t}, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\treturn n.Config.Provider\n\t}\n\tif n.storedProviderConfig.Provider.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn n.storedProviderConfig.Provider\n\t}\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) > 0 {\n\t\t// The import targets should either all be defined via config or none\n\t\t// of them should be. They should also all have the same provider, so it\n\t\t// shouldn't matter which we check here, as they'll all give the same.\n\t\tif n.importTargets[0].Config != nil {\n\t\t\treturn n.importTargets[0].Config.Provider\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn addrs.ImpliedProviderForUnqualifiedType(n.Addr.Resource.ImpliedProvider())\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) ProvisionedBy() []string {\n\t// If we have no configuration, then we have no provisioners\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the list of provisioners we need based on the configuration.\n\t// It is okay to have duplicates here.\n\tresult := make([]string, len(n.Config.Managed.Provisioners))\n\tfor i, p := range impl.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) DotNode(name string, opts *dag.DotOpts) *dag.DotNode {\n\treturn &dag.DotNode{\n\t\tName: name,\n\t\tAttrs: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"label\": n.Name(),\n\t\t\t\"shape\": \"box\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// recordResourceData records some metadata for the resource as a whole in\n// various locations. This currently includes adding resource expansion info to\n// the instance expander, and recording the provider used in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) recordResourceData(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\t// We'll record our expansion decision in the shared \"expander\" object\n\t// so that later operations (i.e. DynamicExpand and expression evaluation)\n\t// can refer to it. Since this node represents the abstract module, we need\n\t// to expand the module here to create all resources.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\n\t// Allowing unknown values in count and for_each is a top-level plan option.\n\t//\n\t// If this is false then the codepaths that handle unknown values below\n\t// become unreachable, because the evaluate functions will reject unknown\n\t// values as an error.\n\tallowUnknown := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.Count != nil:\n\t\tcount, countDiags := evaluateCountExpression(n.Config.Count, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(countDiags)\n\t\tif countDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif count >= 0 {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCount(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, count)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// -1 represents \"unknown\"\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceCountUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tcase n.Config != nil && n.Config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\tforEach, known, forEachDiags := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// This method takes care of all of the business logic of updating this\n\t\t// while ensuring that any existing instances are preserved, etc.\n\t\tif known {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEach(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource, forEach)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\texpander.SetResourceForEachUnknown(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\t}\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\texpander.SetResourceSingle(addr.Module, n.Addr.Resource)\n\t}\n\n\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources are not included in the state\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tstate.SetResourceProvider(addr, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceState reads the current object for a specific instance in\n// the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: reading state for %s\", addr)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceState: no state present for %s\", addr)\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\", addr))\n\t}\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn obj, diags\n}\n\n// readResourceInstanceStateDeposed reads the deposed object for a specific\n// instance in the state.\nfunc (n *NodeAbstractResource) readResourceInstanceStateDeposed(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, key states.DeposedKey) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif key == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"readResourceInstanceStateDeposed used with no instance key; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\"))\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: reading state for %s deposed object %s\", addr, key)\n\n\tsrc := ctx.State().ResourceInstanceObject(addr, key)\n\tif src == nil {\n\t\t// Presumably we only have deposed objects, then.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] readResourceInstanceStateDeposed: no state present for %s deposed object %s\", addr, key)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tschema, currentVersion := (providerSchema).SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.Resource.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Shouldn't happen since we should've failed long ago if no schema is present\n\t\treturn nil, diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"no schema available for %s while reading state; this is a bug in Terraform and should be reported\", addr))\n\n\t}\n\n\tsrc, upgradeDiags := upgradeResourceState(addr, provider, src, schema, currentVersion)\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tupgradeDiags = upgradeDiags.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, addr.String())\n\t}\n\tdiags = diags.Append(upgradeDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Note that we don't have any channel to return warnings here. We'll\n\t\t// accept that for now since warnings during a schema upgrade would\n\t\t// be pretty weird anyway, since this operation is supposed to seem\n\t\t// invisible to the user.\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tobj, err := src.Decode(schema.ImpliedType())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn obj, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule is an\n// annoyingly-task-specific helper function that returns true if and only if\n// the following conditions hold:\n//   - Both of the given vertices represent specific resource instances, as\n//     opposed to unexpanded resources or any other non-resource-related object.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances belong\n//     to the same static module.\n//   - The module instance addresses for both of the resource instances are\n//     not equal, indicating that they belong to different instances of the\n//     same module.\n//\n// This result can be used as a way to compensate for the effects of\n// conservative analysis passes in our graph builders which make their\n// decisions based only on unexpanded addresses, often so that they can behave\n// correctly for interactions between expanded and not-yet-expanded objects.\n//\n// Callers of this helper function will typically skip adding an edge between\n// the two given nodes if this function returns true.\nfunc graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(a, b dag.Vertex) bool {\n\taRI, aOK := a.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tbRI, bOK := b.(GraphNodeResourceInstance)\n\tif !(aOK && bOK) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\taModInst := aRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\tbModInst := bRI.ResourceInstanceAddr().Module\n\taMod := aModInst.Module()\n\tbMod := bModInst.Module()\n\tif !aMod.Equal(bMod) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn !aModInst.Equal(bModInst)\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2676,"cache_read_input_tokens":106262,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2676},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LP7ur8eoW29Egu5fNJWuMc","session_id":"6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c","uuid":"fafe484f-2bac-453e-9345-b7f54d9c33cc"}

It would be preferable to entirely disallow self references\n\t// without the `self` identifier, but those were allowed in provisioners\n\t// for compatibility with legacy configuration. We also can't always just\n\t// filter them out for all resource node types, because the only method we\n\t// have for catching certain invalid configurations are the cycles that\n\t// result from these inter-instance references.\n\treturn filterSelfRefs(n.Addr.Resource, refs)\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\treturn n.dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx)\n\t}\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, module)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)))\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil, diags\n}\n\n// We need to expand the ephemeral resources mostly the same as we do during\n// planning. There a lot of options than happen during planning which aren't\n// applicable to apply however, and we have to make sure we don't re-register\n// checks which already recorded in the plan, so we create a pared down version\n// of the plan expansion here.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) dynamicExpandEphemeral(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tvar g Graph\n\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tresAddr *nodeExpandApplyableResource) expandEphemeralResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, g *Graph) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// The rest of resAddr.Module)\n\n\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n\t// to work below.\n\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\texpander := {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandApplyableResource) ephemeralResourceInstanceSubgraph(addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tconcreteEphemeral := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) It will usually be\n// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\n// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\n// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\ntype NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n\n\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\n\n\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance   = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeCreator            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDeposer            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\n// GraphNodeCreator\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) CreateAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()\n\n\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\n\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\n\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \"count\" set (by reducing\n\t// the count).\n\t//\n\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\n\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\n\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\n\t// plan will use the old values.\n\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tfor _, ref := range ret {\n\t\t\tswitch tr := ref.Subject.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ret\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.Dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no config, and there is no change, then we have nothing\n\t\t// to do and the change was left in the plan for informational\n\t\t// purposes only.\n\t\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\t\tcsrc := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), addrs.NotDeposed)\n\t\tif csrc == nil || csrc.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] NodeApplyableResourceInstance: No config or planned change recorded for %s\", n.Addr)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource node has no configuration attached\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"The graph node for %s has no configuration attached to it. This suggests a bug in Terraform's apply graph builder; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n\tswitch n.Config.Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\t_, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\n\t\taddr:           n.Addr,\n\t\tconfig:         n.Config,\n\t\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t})\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchange, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\t// Stop early if we don't actually have a diff\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif change.Action != plans.Read && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", change.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// In this particular call to applyDataSource we include our planned\n\t// change, which signals that we expect this read to complete fully\n\t// with no unknown values; it'll produce an error if not.\n\tstate, repeatData, applyDiags := n.applyDataSource(ctx, change)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// If n.applyDataSource returned a nil state object with no accompanying\n\t\t// errors then it determined that the given change doesn't require\n\t\t// actually reading the data (e.g. because it was already read during\n\t\t// the plan phase) and so we're only running through here to get the\n\t\t// extra details like precondition/postcondition checks.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\"))\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\n\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(),\n\t\trepeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Declare a bunch of variables that are used for state during\n\t// evaluation. Most of this are written to by-address below.\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\tvar createBeforeDestroyEnabled bool\n\tvar deposedKey states.DeposedKey\n\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff for apply\n\tdiffApply, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// We don't want to do any destroys\n\t// (these are handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance instead)\n\tif diffApply == nil || diffApply.Action == plans.Delete {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.Read {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", diffApply.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\tdestroy := (diffApply.Action == plans.Delete || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\t// Get the stored action for CBD if we have a plan already\n\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = diffApply.Change.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\n\n\tif destroy && n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = true\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled {\n\t\tstate := ctx.State()\n\t\tif n.PreallocatedDeposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = state.DeposeResourceInstanceObject(n.Addr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = n.PreallocatedDeposedKey\n\t\t\tstate.DeposeResourceInstanceObjectForceKey(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: prior object for %s now deposed with key %s\", n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr())\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff\n\tdiff, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Make a new diff, in case we've learned new values in the state\n\t// during apply which we can now incorporate.\n\tdiffApply, _, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(ctx, diff, state, false, n.forceReplace)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource deferred during apply, but not during plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where a provider would mark the resource %q as deferred during apply, but not during plan. This is most likely a bug in the provider. Please file an issue with the provider.\", n.Addr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Compare the diffs\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPlannedChange(ctx, diff, diffApply, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiffApply = reducePlan(addr, diffApply, false)\n\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\n\t// into a NoOp if it only requires destroying, since destroying\n\t// is handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance. If so, we'll\n\t// still run through most of the logic here because we do still\n\t// need to deal with other book-keeping such as marking the\n\t// change as \"complete\", and running the author's postconditions.\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, diffApply))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is no change, there was nothing to apply, and we don't need to\n\t// re-write the state, but we do need to re-evaluate postconditions.\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n\t}\n\n\tstate, applyDiags := n.apply(ctx, state, diffApply, n.Config, repeatData, n.CreateBeforeDestroy())\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\n\t// We clear the change out here so that future nodes don't see a change\n\t// that is already complete.\n\terr = n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// dependencies are always updated to match the configuration during apply\n\t\tstate.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\n\t}\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run Provisioners\n\tcreateNew := (diffApply.Action == plans.Create || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, createNew, configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate)\n\t// the provisioner errors count as port of the apply error, so we can bundle the diags\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled && diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t// This should never happen, and so it always indicates a bug.\n\t\t\t// We should evaluate this node only if we've previously deposed\n\t\t\t// an object as part of the same operation.\n\t\t\tif diffApply != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This occurred during a %s action. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourcePostconditions(ctx EvalContext, repeatData instances.RepetitionData) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\treturn diags.Append(checkDiags)\n}\n\n// checkPlannedChange produces errors if the _actual_ expected value is not\n// compatible with what was recorded in the plan.\n//\n// Errors here are most often indicative of a bug in the provider, so our error\n// messages will report with that in mind. It's also possible that there's a bug\n// in Terraform's Core's own \"proposed new value\" code in EvalDiff.\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) checkPlannedChange(ctx EvalContext, plannedChange, actualChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support %q\", addr.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] checkPlannedChange: Verifying that actual change (action %s) matches planned change (action %s)\", actualChange.Action, plannedChange.Action)\n\n\tif plannedChange.Action != actualChange.Action {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase plannedChange.Action == plans.Update && actualChange.Action == plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// It's okay for an update to become a NoOp once we've filled in\n\t\t\t// all of the unknown values, since the final values might actually\n\t\t\t// match what was there before after all.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] After incorporating new values learned so far during apply, %s change has become NoOp\", absAddr)\n\n\t\tcase (plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete && actualChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate) ||\n\t\t\t(plannedChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate && actualChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete):\n\t\t\t// If the order of replacement changed, then that is a bug in terraform\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, the planned action changed from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, plannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q changed the planned action from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tplannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\terrs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, plannedChange.After, actualChange.After)\n\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q produced an invalid new value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// maybeTainted takes the resource addr, new value, planned change, and possible\n// error from an apply operation and return a new instance object marked as\n// tainted if it appears that a create operation has failed.\nfunc maybeTainted(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, err error) *states.ResourceInstanceObject {\n\tif state == nil || change == nil || err == nil {\n\t\treturn state\n\t}\n\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s was already tainted, so nothing to do\", addr)\n\t\treturn state\n\t}\n\tif change.Action == plans.Create {\n\t\t// If there are errors during a _create_ then the object is\n\t\t// in an undefined state, and so we'll mark it as tainted so\n\t\t// we can try again on the next run.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We don't do this for other change actions because errors\n\t\t// during updates will often not change the remote object at all.\n\t\t// If there _were_ changes prior to the error, it's the provider's\n\t\t// responsibility to record the effect of those changes in the\n\t\t// object value it returned.\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] maybeTainted: %s encountered an error during creation, so it is now marked as tainted\", addr)\n\t\treturn state.AsTainted()\n\t}\n\treturn state\n}\nENDFILE\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1910,"cache_read_input_tokens":115646,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1910},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LP7ur8eoW29Egu5fNJWuMc","session_id":"6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c","uuid":"9b3b6783-5249-460a-8eb2-e0075645fcd3"}

(\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\ntype DiffTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tState    *states.State\n\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\n\tConfig   *configs.Config\n}\n\n// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\n// Postconditions defined in the configuration.\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\n\t// unit tests may have no config\n\tif t.Config == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\n\tif cfg == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\n\tif res == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\n}\n\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\n\t\t// Nothing to do!\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\")\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tstate := t.State\n\tchanges := t.Changes\n\n\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\n\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\n\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\n\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\n\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\n\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\n\t\taddr := rc.Addr\n\t\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\n\n\t\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\n\t\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\n\t\t// other actions.\n\t\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\n\t\tswitch rc.Action {\n\t\tcase plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\n\t\t\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\n\t\t\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\n\t\t\t// results of other changes.\n\t\t\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\n\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\n\t\tcase plans.Forget:\n\t\t\tforget = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\n\t\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\n\t\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\n\t\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\", addr, dk),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\n\t\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\n\t\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\n\t\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\n\t\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\n\t\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\n\t\t\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\n\t\t\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\n\t\t\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\n\t\t\t// object we expect.\n\t\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\t\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\tif ris == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\n\t\t\t\t\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\", addr),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\n\t\t\t\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\n\t\t\t\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\n\t\t\t\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\n\t\t\t\t// between now and then.\n\t\t\t\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\n\t\t\t\t// DeposedKey.\n\t\t\t\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif update {\n\t\t\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\n\t\t\t\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\n\t\t\t\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\n\t\t\t\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\n\t\t\t\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\n\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif delete {\n\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif forget {\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\")\n\n\treturn diags.Err()\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3745,"cache_read_input_tokens":123736,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3745},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LP7ur8eoW29Egu5fNJWuMc","session_id":"6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c","uuid":"fee8b0f5-fa38-4f24-a44b-23a5e6c61754"}

The determination of this depends\n// on the count argument given.\n//\n// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.\n// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count\n// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.\ntype OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\n\tAddr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans\n\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config\n\tState         *states.State               // Full global state\n}\n\nfunc (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\trs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)\n\tif rs == nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!\n\t}\n\n\t// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the\n\t// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any\n\t// reasonable Terraform configuration.\nHave:\n\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthisAddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\tfor _, wantAddr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n\t\t\tif wantAddr.Equal(thisAddr) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue Have\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"\n\n\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)\n\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\tif f := t.Concrete;
