{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_014KP4aBqJvzsJzopmyiFgAw","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01A8kmadX4bxM7tLsYW7XYnz","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_apply.go","content":"// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// ApplyGraphBuilder implements GraphBuilder and is responsible for building\n// a graph for applying a Terraform diff.\n//\n// Because the graph is built from the diff (vs. the config or state),\n// this helps ensure that the apply-time graph doesn't modify any resources\n// that aren't explicitly in the diff. There are other scenarios where the\n// diff can be deviated, so this is just one layer of protection.\ntype ApplyGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree that the diff was built from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// Changes describes the changes that we need apply.\n\tChanges *plans.ChangesSrc\n\n\t// DeferredChanges describes the changes that were deferred during the plan\n\t// and should not be applied.\n\tDeferredChanges []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the root module input variables captured as\n\t// part of the plan object, which we must reproduce in the apply step\n\t// to get a consistent result.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of the plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target. This is only required to make sure\n\t// unnecessary outputs aren't included in the apply graph. The plan\n\t// builder successfully handles targeting resources. In the future,\n\t// outputs should go into the diff so that this is unnecessary.\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are the resource instance addresses that the user\n\t// requested to force replacement for when creating the plan, if any.\n\t// The apply step refers to these as part of verifying that the planned\n\t// actions remain consistent between plan and apply.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"ApplyGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *ApplyGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\t// Custom factory for creating providers.\n\tconcreteProvider := func(a *NodeAbstractProvider) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableProvider{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractProvider: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &nodeExpandApplyableResource{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: a,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconcreteResourceInstance := func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn &NodeApplyableResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\t\tforceReplace:                 b.ForceReplace,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config. During apply,\n\t\t// we use this just to ensure that the whole-resource metadata is\n\t\t// updated to reflect things such as whether the count argument is\n\t\t// set in config, or which provider configuration manages each resource.\n\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add dynamic values\n\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{},\n\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:     b.Config,\n\t\t\tDestroying: b.Operation == walkDestroy,\n\t\t\tOverrides:  b.Overrides,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Creates all the resource instances represented in the diff, along\n\t\t// with dependency edges against the whole-resource nodes added by\n\t\t// ConfigTransformer above.\n\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n\t\t\tState:    b.State,\n\t\t\tChanges:  b.Changes,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Creates nodes for all the deferred changes.\n\t\t&DeferredTransformer{\n\t\t\tDeferredChanges: b.DeferredChanges,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add nodes and edges for check block assertions. Check block data\n\t\t// sources were added earlier.\n\t\t&checkTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:    b.Config,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Create orphan output nodes\n\t\t&OrphanOutputTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Attach the configuration to any resources\n\t\t&AttachResourceConfigTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// add providers\n\t\ttransformProviders(concreteProvider, b.Config, b.ExternalProviderConfigs),\n\n\t\t// Remove modules no longer present in the config\n\t\t&RemovedModuleTransformer{Config: b.Config, State: b.State},\n\n\t\t// Must attach schemas before ReferenceTransformer so that we can\n\t\t// analyze the configuration to find references.\n\t\t&AttachSchemaTransformer{Plugins: b.Plugins, Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Create expansion nodes for all of the module calls. This must\n\t\t// come after all other transformers that create nodes representing\n\t\t// objects that can belong to modules.\n\t\t&ModuleExpansionTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\n\t\t// Plug in any external references.\n\t\t&ExternalReferenceTransformer{\n\t\t\tExternalReferences: b.ExternalReferences,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Nested data blocks should be loaded after every other resource has\n\t\t// done its thing.\n\t\t&checkStartTransformer{Config: b.Config, Operation: b.Operation},\n\n\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during apply.\n\t\t//\n\t\t// FIXME: this should not need to be recalculated during apply.\n\t\t// Currently however, the instance object which stores the planned\n\t\t// information is lost for newly created instances because it contains\n\t\t// no state value, and we end up recalculating CBD for all nodes.\n\t\t&ForcedCBDTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Destruction ordering\n\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tChanges:   b.Changes,\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&CBDEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig: b.Config,\n\t\t\tState:  b.State,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// In a destroy, we need to remove configuration nodes that are not used\n\t\t// at all, as they may not be able to evaluate. These include variables,\n\t\t// locals, and instance expanders.\n\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Target\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Close any ephemeral resource instances.\n\t\t&ephemeralResourceCloseTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Close opened plugin connections\n\t\t&CloseProviderTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// close the root module\n\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Perform the transitive reduction to make our graph a bit\n\t\t// more understandable if possible (it usually is possible).\n\t\t&TransitiveReductionTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\treturn steps\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":387,"cache_read_input_tokens":139636,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":387},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LP7ur8eoW29Egu5fNJWuMc","session_id":"6f353274-00a0-47ba-83a1-0d1cdbbca63c","uuid":"24bbc741-24fc-48b4-a048-3f221b4933f4"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_019uYKAgKVWZLqwrcniHArzk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017MFmTSvwKDKa4eUoyspgyR","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/internal/terraform/graph_builder_plan.go","content":"// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for\n// planning and for other \"plan-like\" operations which don't require an\n// already-calculated plan as input.\n//\n// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:\n//\n//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which\n//     represents the desired state.\n//\n//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because\n//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning\n//     to take against a particular resource instance.\ntype PlanGraphBuilder struct {\n\t// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.\n\tConfig *configs.Config\n\n\t// State is the current state\n\tState *states.State\n\n\t// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables\n\t// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part\n\t// of the plan walk.\n\tRootVariableValues InputValues\n\n\t// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider\n\t// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available\n\t// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit\n\t// initialization step.\n\tExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface\n\n\t// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and\n\t// provisioners) available for use.\n\tPlugins *contextPlugins\n\n\t// Targets are resources to target\n\tTargets []addrs.Targetable\n\n\t// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have\n\t// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace\n\t// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.\n\tForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources\n\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\t// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which\n\t// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the\n\t// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing\n\t// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to\n\t// resource instances. (This is for the \"refresh only\" planning mode,\n\t// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)\n\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\tConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n\tConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc\n\n\t// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.\n\tOperation walkOperation\n\n\t// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to\n\t// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within\n\t// the actual graph.\n\tExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference\n\n\t// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing\n\t// framework.\n\tOverrides *mocking.Overrides\n\n\t// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.\n\tImportTargets []*ImportTarget\n\n\t// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed\n\t// from state without destroying.\n\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n\n\t// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for\n\t// any import targets that do not already have configuration.\n\t//\n\t// If empty, then config will not be generated.\n\tGenerateConfigPath string\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] building graph for %s\", b.Operation)\n\treturn (&BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: b.Steps(),\n\t\tName:  \"PlanGraphBuilder\",\n\t}).Build(path)\n}\n\n// See GraphBuilder\nfunc (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {\n\tswitch b.Operation {\n\tcase walkPlan:\n\t\tb.initPlan()\n\tcase walkPlanDestroy:\n\t\tb.initDestroy()\n\tcase walkValidate:\n\t\tb.initValidate()\n\tcase walkImport:\n\t\tb.initImport()\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(\"invalid plan operation: \" + b.Operation.String())\n\t}\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Creates all the resources represented in the config\n\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: b.ConcreteResource,\n\t\t\tConfig:   b.Config,\n\t\t\tdestroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\n\t\t\timportTargets: b.ImportTargets,\n\n\t\t\t// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.\n\t\t\tgenerateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add dynamic values\n\t\t&RootVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tRawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&ModuleVariableTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:       b.Config,\n\t\t\tPlanning:     true,\n\t\t\tDestroyApply: false, // always false for planning\n\t\t},\n\t\t&variableValidationTransformer{\n\t\t\tvalidateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,\n\t\t},\n\t\t&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},\n\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\tConfig:      b.Config,\n\t\t\tRefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,\n\t\t\tDestroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t\tOverrides:   b.Overrides,\n\n\t\t\t// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph\n\t\t\t// builder as \"planning\" for our purposes here, because we share\n\t\t\t// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk\n\t\t\t// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are\n\t\t\t// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.\n\t\t\tPlanning: true,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. configuration\n\t\t&attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// DestroyEdgeTransformer is only required during a plan so that the\n\t\t// TargetsTransformer can determine which nodes to keep in the graph.\n\t\t&DestroyEdgeTransformer{\n\t\t\tOperation: b.Operation,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{\n\t\t\tskip: b.Operation != walkPlanDestroy,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Target\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: b.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Detect when create_before_destroy must be forced on for a particular\n\t\t// node due to dependency edges, to avoid graph cycles during \\tvar g Graph\\n341: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n342: \\n343: \\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\\n344: \\n345: \\tfor _, res := range orphans {\\n346: \\t\\tfor key := range \\n387: \\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\\n388: \\n389: \\treturn &g, diags\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: // expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\\n393: // itself in the context of a particular module instance.\\n394: //\\n395: // It has several side-effects:\\n396: //   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\\n397: //   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \\\"expander\\\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\\n398: //   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\\n399: //\\n400: // After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\\n401: // within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\\n402: // checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\\n403: // object instances for this resource instance.\\n404: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n405: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n406: \\n407: \\t// The rest of our work here resAddr.Module)\\n410: \\n411: \\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\\n412: \\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\\n413: \\t// to work below.\\n414: \\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\\n415: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n416: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n417: \\t\\treturn nil, \\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\\n423: \\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\\n424: \\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\\n425: \\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\\n426: \\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\\n427: \\n428: \\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\\n429: \\t// an function we're about to call, because\\n441: \\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\\n442: \\n443: \\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\\n444: \\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\\n445: \\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\\n446: \\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges \\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\\n456: }\\n457: \\n458: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n459: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n460: \\n461: \\tif n.Config == nil && Since they're new\n// resources they don't depend on anything else and refreshes are isolated\n// so this is nearly a perfect use case for dynamic expand.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tg := &Graph{Path: ctx.Path()}\n\n\t// nameCounter is used to de-dup names in the state.\n\tnameCounter := make(map[string]int)\n\n\t// Compile the list of addresses that we'll be inserting into the state.\n\t// We do this ahead of time so we can verify that we aren't importing\n\t// something that already exists.\n\taddrs := make([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, len(n.states))\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\taddr := n.Addr\n\t\tif t := state.TypeName; t != \"\" {\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Type = t\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Determine if we need to suffix the name to de-dup\n\t\tkey := addr.String()\n\t\tcount, ok := nameCounter[key]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Name += fmt.Sprintf(\"-%d\", count)\n\t\t}\n\t\tnameCounter[key] = count\n\n\t\t// Add it to our list\n\t\taddrs[i] = addr\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify that all the addresses are clear\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n\t\texisting := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\tif existing != nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Resource already managed by Terraform\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform is already managing a remote object for %s. Since DynamicExpand is always called after Execute, this is\n\t// safe.\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\tg.Add(&graphNodeImportStateSub{\n\t\t\tTargetAddr:       addrs[i],\n\t\t\tState:            state,\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(g)\n\n\t// Done!\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodeImportStateSub is the sub-node of graphNodeImportState\n// and is part of the subgraph. *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n340: \\tvar g Graph\\n341: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n342: \\n343: \\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\\n344: \\n345: \\tfor _, res := range orphans {\\n346: \\t\\tfor key := range \\n387: \\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\\n388: \\n389: \\treturn &g, diags\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: // expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\\n393: // itself in the context of a particular module instance.\\n394: //\\n395: // It has several side-effects:\\n396: //   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\\n397: //   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \\\"expander\\\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\\n398: //   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\\n399: //\\n400: // After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\\n401: // within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\\n402: // checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\\n403: // object instances for this resource instance.\\n404: func (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n405: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n406: \\n407: \\t// The rest of our work here \\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\n\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\n\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\n\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\n\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\n\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\n\t\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\n\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\n\t// deleting a resource.\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Expand the current module.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\n\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\n\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\n\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\n\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\n\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\n\t// handle.\n\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\n\t\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\t\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\t\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\t\treturn g, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\n\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\n\t// not have any unknown imports.\n\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tpanic(\"unexpected unknown imports\")\n\t}\n\n\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// Import blocks are expanded in conjunction with their associated resource block.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceImports(ctx EvalContext, allowUnknown bool) (addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Imports maps the target address to an import ID.\n\tknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]()\n\tunknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]()\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) == 0 {\n\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Import blocks are only valid within the root module, and must be\n\t// evaluated within that context\n\tctx = evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\n\tfor _, imp := range n.importTargets {\n\t\tif imp.Config == nil {\n\t\t\t// if we have a legacy addr, it was cty.StringVal(imp.IDString))\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// legacy import tests may have no configuration\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no configuration for := addrs.ParseAbsResourceInstance(traversal)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tds)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, to, imp.Config.To))\n\n\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey, allowUnknown)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tknownImports.Put(to, importID)\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: found single import target %s\", to)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tforEachData, known, forEachDiags := newForEachEvaluator(imp.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown).ImportValues()\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !known {\n\t\t\t// Then we need to parse the target address as a PartialResource\n\t\t\t// instead of a known resource.\n\t\t\taddr, evalDiags := evalImportUnknownToExpression(imp.Config.To)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// We're going addr.ConfigResource()\n\t\t\tmodInsts := state.ModuleInstances(cfg.Module)\n\t\t\tfor _, modInst := range modInsts {\n\t\t\t\tabs := cfg.Absolute(modInst)\n\t\t\t\tresource := state.Resource(cfg.Absolute(modInst))\n\t\t\t\tif resource == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Then we are creating every instance of this resource.\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor inst := range resource.Instances {\n\t\t\t\t\tknownInstances.Add(abs.Instance(inst))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tunknownImports.Put(addr, knownInstances)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, keyData := range forEachData {\n\t\t\tres, evalDiags := evalImportToExpression(imp.Config.To, keyData)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, res, imp.Config.To))\n\n\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, keyData, allowUnknown)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tknownImports.Put(res, importID)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: expanded import target %s\", res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// filter out any known import which already exist in state\n\tfor _, el := range knownImports.Elements() {\n\t\tif state.ResourceInstance(el.Key) != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] expandResourceImports: skipping import address %s already in state\", el.Key)\n\t\t\tknownImports.Remove(el.Key)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n}\n\n// validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\n// a configured instance.\n//\n// This function is only called from within the dynamicExpand method, the\n// import validation is inlined within the dynamicExpandPartial method for the\n// alternate code path.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tfor _, addr := range expandedImports.Keys() {\n\t\tif !expandedInstances.Has(addr) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath == \"\" && len(n.importTargets) == 1 {\n\t\t\t// we have no config and aren't generating any. If we\n\t\t\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\n\t\t\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n\n\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\n\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n\n\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n\t\t\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\n\t\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsMgr.Unlock()\n\n\treturn orphans\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar g Graph\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n\n\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n\t// instances of the module.\n\t//\n\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n\n\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n\n\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n\t// visiting the checks.\n\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n\n\treturn &g, diags\n}\n\n// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n//\n// It has several side-effects:\n//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n//\n// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n// object instances for this resource instance.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n\n\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n\t// to work below.\n\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n\n\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n\t// an index.\n\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n\t// instance address has one.\n\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n\t}\n\tif mustHaveIndex {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n\t}\n\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n\n\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\n\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full    state,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n\n\t\t// Targeting\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\n\n\t\t// If we're in
